title: The Defiance of Marjane Satrapi
description: The author of the best-selling graphic memoirPersepolis, who died last week, made rebellion into a lifelong project.
author: Hillary Chute

title: What Donald Trump Will Never Understand About Fighting
description: Mixed martial arts has a lot to teach the president.
author: Bhumika Tharoor

title: The Betrayal of Black Patriots
description: They devoted their lives to serving the United States. Now the nation’s top military leader is sending the message that they’re not welcome.
author: Clint Smith

title: The Americans Shelling Out Five Figures for a Coat of Arms
description: A nation founded on the rejection of aristocratic traditions still yearns for them.
author: Helen Lewis

title: Why the Public Is Gravitating Toward the Hunter Biden Approach
description: His return to public life is going much better than Jill’s.
author: David A. Graham

title: Why Republicans Aren’t Condemning Trump’sMeet the PressWalkout
description: Denying the legitimacy of vote-counting has become party doctrine.
author: Jonathan Chait

title: AtlanticTrivia: Skulduggery and Hijinks
description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
author: Tom Nichols

title: The Unglamorous Truth About the Average Tradwife
description: Most stay-at-home moms simply can’t afford child care.
author: Faith Hill

title: The White Identitarians Are Having a Moment
description: Now that DEI and anti-racism are in retreat, they’re moving on to a more ambitious goal.
author: Thomas Chatterton Williams

title: The Two Kinds of American Patriotism
description: As our nation turns 250, it’s worth asking how best to love one’s country.
author: Gal Beckerman

title: The ‘Battle Hymn’ Can’t Be Ignored
description: The rousing urgency of the song seems to possess a power that, according to its own lyrics, “transfigures you and me.”
author: Jake Lundberg

title: America’s Promise
description: Julia Ward Howe’s “Battle Hymn of the Republic” continues to inspire a nation to realize its ideals.
author: Jeffrey Goldberg

title: How America Gave Up on Its Own History
description: Unable to agree on how to interpret the American story, the country’s schools, universities, and political institutions have stopped trying to tell it at all.

title: The Absurd World Cup
description: FIFA’s president calls football a great unifier, but is it a match for the chaos of 2026?

title: The Road Wound Upward

title: The Liberal District That Could Oust a Trump-Defying Democrat
description: A New York City congressional race shows the fractures on the left.

title: Flu Vaccines Should Not Be This Hard
description: Normally, the CDC’s vaccine advisors weigh in on flu vaccines in June. This year, the panel is in disarray.
author: Katherine J. Wu

title: Ukraine Is Not Losing. Russia Is Not Winning.
description: A momentum shift that changes everything
author: Anne Applebaum

title: Cut Platner Loose
description: Just a few years ago, Republicans sacrificed an Alabama Senate seat rather than endure an unacceptable colleague. Will Democrats do the same in Maine?

title: The Republicans Defying Trump
description: Panelists joined to discuss why the president could face pushback from soon-to-be former senators.
author: The Editors
